item.title: Quantum scale-up Pasqal plans $2B SPAC listing, promises to ‘remain French’
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: A SPAC deal that is set to take French quantum company Pasqal public on the Nasdaq values the company at $2 billion pre-money.

item.title: India’s Karnataka signals intent to ban social media for under-16s
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Karnataka's proposal highlights a growing global debate over children’s rights and online policy.

item.title: After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.

item.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: Cluely CEO Roy Lee admits to publicly lying about revenue numbers last year
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The $7 million in annual recurring revenue that Cluely CEO Roy Lee shared last summer was a lie, its founder and CEO Roy Lee admitted on Thursday on X.

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

item.title: FBI investigating hack on its wiretap and surveillance systems: Report
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Hackers allegedly broke into the FBI’s networks, according to a report by CNN.

item.title: US reportedly considering sweeping new chip export controls
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: In an alleged drafted proposal, the U.S. government would play a role in every chip export sale regardless of which country it's coming from.

item.title: Amazon is rolling out a redesigned Fire TV app
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: With the updated Fire TV app, users can browse and discover content directly from their phone, manage their watchlist on the go, and play titles on their TV from the mobile app.

item.title: BYD rolls out EV batteries with 5-minute ‘flash charging’ — but there’s a catch
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The Chinese automaker's new Blade Battery 2.0 is capable of charging at up to 1.5 megawatts, dramatically slashing the time it takes to fill up.

item.title: Italian prosecutors confirm journalist was hacked with Paragon spyware
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Italian authorities are making progress in their investigation into a wide-ranging spyware scandal in Italy involving Paragon spyware. But the mystery of who hacked two Italian journalists with Paragon spyware continues.

item.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

item.title: X revamps Creator Subscriptions with new features, like exclusive threads and shareable cards
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X is rolling out a series of new features for creators, like the ability to offer paid threads and other marketing tools.

item.title: Hardware testing startup Nominal hits $1B valuation, raises $155M in 10 months
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Nominal provides hardware testing equipment to defense tech companies. Founders Fund led the preemptive deal.

item.title: Roblox launches real-time AI chat rephrasing to filter out banned language
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The new feature goes beyond Roblox’s current text filter, which simply replaces banned words and phrases with the “#” symbol.

item.title: FYI: Impersonators are (still) targeting companies with fake TechCrunch outreach
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Staff
item.description: Fraudsters are impersonating TechCrunch reporters and event leads, and reaching out to companies. Here's what we're doing about it, and what you can look out for.

item.title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

item.title: Google says half of all zero-days it tracked in 2025 targeted buggy enterprise tech
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Enterprise software was a major focus of zero-day activity during 2025, with security and networking devices, like firewalls, VPNs, and virtualization platforms among the most targeted by malicious hackers.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: GPT-5.4 is billed as "our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work."
